transcript.interviewer: How would you describe how you make decisions?
transcript.interviewee: I think of myself as sitting between two poles — security on one side, adventure on the other. I know roughly where I sit on that axis.
transcript.interviewer: Where do you sit? That's the hard part. Most days I'd say I'm a little toward the security side. But it doesn't feel settled — it feels like I'm being pulled, like the needle won't hold still. Pulled how? There's a real tension. Part of me wants the safe, mapped life and part of me wants to burn it down and travel. Both options are right there in front of me and I keep reaching for one button, then the other. Is it a position you've found, or a choice you haven't made? Honestly I can't tell. Some days it feels like coordinates — I'm just a cautious-ish person and that's my spot. Other days it feels like an unresolved decision tearing at me, two buttons I can't pick between. If you had to commit to a frame? I'd say I'm located on an axis, but standing on a part of it that shakes. It's a position and a pull at the same time. I'm placed, but not at peace with the placement.
